_note: Artem russian_trap b01-b02 — focused drill on the look_for / look_after PV pair confusion surfaced in 2026-05-03 A1-A2 production test (item #4: 'looked after for keys'). Two items, one each direction of the contrast: 'искать' → look for (NOT look after), and 'присматривать (за)' → look after (NOT look for). Both directions trap the same particle-meaning swap. Short prompts, biz/family context.

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'искать' = search → English 'look FOR' (NOT 'look after'). The look_for/look_after pair shares base verb 'look' but particles split into opposite domains: 'for' = search, 'after' = take care of. Failure mode: blending into 'look after for X'.
correct_answers: I am looking for my phone
I am looking for my phone.

common_errors.pattern_label: look after (caregiving) instead of look for (search)
common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Look after' = take care of (children, pets, the office while away). For 'искать' = search → 'look FOR'. ✓ 'looking FOR my phone'. The two PVs are a particle-swap trap: same base 'look', opposite meanings. Don't blend them into 'look after for'.
✗ 'Look after for' is a blend of two different PVs. Pick one: 'look FOR' (search) OR 'look after' (take care of). Russian 'искать' = search → only 'look FOR'.
✗ 'Looking my phone' — 'look' as a search verb needs 'for'. ✓ 'looking FOR my phone'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'I'm looking for my phone.' Russian 'искать' always maps to 'look FOR' — never 'look after', never bare 'look'.

exercises.calque_trap: Russian 'присматривать за' / 'ухаживать за' = take care of → English 'look AFTER' (NOT 'look for'). Mirror of rt_artem_b01: same base verb, opposite particle. Locks the contrast in both directions.
exercises.prompt_ru: Кто будет присматривать за детьми вечером?
correct_answers: Who'll look after the kids in the evening
Who will look after the kids in the evening
Who's going to look after the kids in the evening
Who is going to look after the kids in the evening
Who'll look after the children in the evening
Who will look after the children in the evening

common_errors.pattern_label: look for (search) instead of look after (care)
common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'Look for the kids' = search for them (as if they're missing). For 'присматривать за' = take care of → 'look AFTER'. ✓ 'look AFTER the kids'. This is the mirror of the искать/look-for trap: same base, opposite particle, opposite meaning.
'Watch the kids' is OK informal English (American-leaning), but the standard PV for 'присматривать за' is 'look AFTER'. Reach for the PV first.
✗ 'Take care the kids' — missing 'of'. ✓ 'take care OF the kids'. But the target PV here is the simpler 'look AFTER'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Who'll look after the kids in the evening?' Russian 'присматривать за' always maps to 'look AFTER' — never 'look for' (that's search).
